title: Booking a Doctor's Appointment
summary: A patient calls a doctor's clinic to book an appointment. The receptionist asks about her symptoms and availability and finds a suitable time for her to come in.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Good morning, Greenwood Medical Centre. How can I help you?

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: Hello, I'd like to make an appointment to see a doctor, please.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Of course. Are you a registered patient with us?

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: Yes, I am. My name is Sarah Browne. B-R-O-W-N-E.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Thank you, I can see your details here. Can I ask what the appointment is for?

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: I've had a sore throat and a headache for the past three days. I'm also feeling quite tired.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Is this urgent, or would a routine appointment be fine?

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: It's not an emergency, but I'd rather not wait too long.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Understood. We have an available slot tomorrow at ten fifteen in the morning. Would that work for you?

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: Tomorrow morning is perfect. Which doctor will I be seeing?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: You'll be seeing Dr Patel. She's very good.

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: That's fine. Is there anything I should bring with me?

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: Just your patient ID card if you have it. If not, we can look you up on the system.

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: Great, I'll bring it. And where exactly is the clinic? I've never been before.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: We're at fourteen Oak Road, just opposite the post office.

body.speaker: Patient
body.text: Perfect. Thank you so much for your help.

body.speaker: Receptionist
body.text: You're welcome. I hope you feel better soon. See you tomorrow at ten fifteen.

annotations.meaningVi: bệnh nhân đã đăng ký (trong hệ thống phòng khám)
annotations.exampleEn: You need to be a registered patient to book an appointment at this clinic.
He has a sore throat and finds it hard to swallow.
I've had this cough for the past three days.
Is it urgent, or can it wait until tomorrow?

annotations.meaningVi: cuộc hẹn khám thông thường (không khẩn cấp)
annotations.exampleEn: She booked a routine appointment for a general check-up.

annotations.phrase: I'd rather not wait too long
annotations.meaningVi: Tôi không muốn chờ quá lâu
annotations.exampleEn: I'd rather not wait too long for the results.

annotations.meaningVi: khung giờ còn trống, suất hẹn còn
annotations.exampleEn: The first available slot is on Thursday afternoon.
Please present your patient ID card at the reception desk.

annotations.phrase: look you up on the system
annotations.meaningVi: tra thông tin của bạn trên hệ thống
annotations.exampleEn: If you don't have your membership number, we can look you up on the system.
The pharmacy is just opposite the train station.

annotations.phrase: I hope you feel better soon
annotations.meaningVi: Tôi hy vọng bạn sớm khỏe lại
annotations.exampleEn: 'I have a cold.' 'Oh no, I hope you feel better soon!'

annotations.meaningVi: Tôi bị đau họng (và vẫn đang bị)
annotations.exampleEn: I've had a headache all morning.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for ongoing conditions: 'I've had a sore throat for three days'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại hoàn thành (have/has + past participle) với 'for' hoặc 'since' để mô tả tình trạng bắt đầu trong quá khứ và vẫn đang tiếp tục. 'I've had a sore throat for the past three days' — Tôi đã bị đau họng trong ba ngày qua (và vẫn đang bị). Đây là cách diễn đạt rất phổ biến khi mô tả triệu chứng bệnh với bác sĩ.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present perfect (have/has + past participle) with 'for' to describe a condition that started in the past and is still continuing. Example: 'I've had a sore throat for the past three days.' This is the standard way to describe symptoms to a doctor. Compare: 'I had a sore throat' (finished) vs 'I've had a sore throat' (still happening).

grammarNotes.title: 'I'd rather' for preferences
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I'd rather + verb (nguyên thể không có to)' diễn tả sở thích — điều bạn muốn hơn. 'I'd rather not wait too long' — Tôi không muốn chờ quá lâu. 'I'd rather see a female doctor' — Tôi muốn gặp bác sĩ nữ hơn. Đây là cách lịch sự, thường dùng trong hội thoại.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'I'd rather + bare infinitive' to express a preference. Example: 'I'd rather not wait too long.' / 'I'd rather see a doctor today.' For a negative preference: 'I'd rather not + verb.' This is more polite than 'I don't want to' and very common in formal or semi-formal situations.

translationVi:
Lễ tân: Xin chào buổi sáng, Trung tâm Y tế Greenwood. Tôi có thể giúp gì cho bạn?
Bệnh nhân: Xin chào, tôi muốn đặt lịch hẹn gặp bác sĩ, xin vui lòng.
Lễ tân: Tất nhiên. Bạn có phải là bệnh nhân đã đăng ký tại đây không?
Bệnh nhân: Có, tôi có. Tên tôi là Sarah Browne. B-R-O-W-N-E.
Lễ tân: Cảm ơn, tôi thấy thông tin của bạn ở đây. Tôi có thể hỏi lý do cuộc hẹn không?
Bệnh nhân: Tôi bị đau họng và đau đầu trong ba ngày qua. Tôi cũng cảm thấy khá mệt mỏi.
Lễ tân: Tôi rất tiếc khi nghe vậy. Đây có phải trường hợp khẩn cấp không, hay cuộc hẹn thông thường là ổn?
Bệnh nhân: Không phải trường hợp khẩn cấp, nhưng tôi không muốn chờ quá lâu.
Lễ tân: Được rồi. Chúng tôi có một khung giờ còn trống vào ngày mai lúc mười giờ mười lăm phút buổi sáng. Thời gian đó có phù hợp với bạn không?
Bệnh nhân: Sáng mai thì hoàn hảo. Tôi sẽ gặp bác sĩ nào vậy?
Lễ tân: Bạn sẽ gặp Bác sĩ Patel. Bà ấy rất tốt.
Bệnh nhân: Được rồi. Tôi có cần mang theo gì không?
Lễ tân: Chỉ cần thẻ bệnh nhân nếu bạn có. Nếu không, chúng tôi có thể tra thông tin của bạn trên hệ thống.
Bệnh nhân: Tốt lắm, tôi sẽ mang theo. Và phòng khám ở đâu chính xác? Tôi chưa đến đây bao giờ.
Lễ tân: Chúng tôi ở số mười bốn đường Oak, ngay đối diện bưu điện.
Bệnh nhân: Hoàn hảo. Cảm ơn bạn rất nhiều đã giúp đỡ.
Lễ tân: Không có gì. Tôi hy vọng bạn sớm khỏe lại. Hẹn gặp ngày mai lúc mười giờ mười lăm phút.

questions.prompt: What is the name of the medical centre?
questions.explanation: The receptionist answers: 'Good morning, Greenwood Medical Centre.'
questions.hint: The receptionist says it in her opening greeting.

questions.explanation: The patient says 'My name is Sarah Browne. B-R-O-W-N-E.'
questions.hint: She spells out her surname carefully.

questions.prompt: What symptoms does the patient have?
options: A cough, a fever, and a rash
A sore throat, a headache, and tiredness
questions.explanation: She says 'I've had a sore throat and a headache for the past three days. I'm also feeling quite tired.'

questions.prompt: How long has she had these symptoms?
questions.explanation: 'I've had a sore throat and a headache for the past three days.'
questions.hint: The duration is mentioned directly after the symptoms.

questions.prompt: Is the patient's situation an emergency?
options: Yes, she needs to be seen immediately
No, but she would rather not wait too long
Yes, she has a high fever
No, she is happy to wait a week
questions.explanation: She says 'It's not an emergency, but I'd rather not wait too long.'
questions.hint: She describes it as not an emergency but still somewhat urgent.

questions.prompt: What time is the available appointment slot?
questions.explanation: The receptionist says 'tomorrow at ten fifteen in the morning.'
questions.hint: The time is given precisely — it is not on the hour.

questions.prompt: Which doctor will the patient see?
questions.hint: The receptionist names the doctor and gives a brief positive comment.

questions.prompt: What does the receptionist tell the patient to bring?
options: A photo ID such as a passport
questions.explanation: 'Just your patient ID card if you have it.'
questions.hint: It is something specifically for the clinic.

questions.prompt: What happens if the patient doesn't have her ID card?
options: She must book a new appointment
She will be asked to pay a fee
They can look her up on the system
questions.explanation: 'If not, we can look you up on the system.'
questions.hint: The receptionist offers a flexible alternative.
A number and a road name are given.

questions.prompt: What landmark is the clinic near?
questions.explanation: The clinic is 'just opposite the post office.'
questions.hint: It is a public building directly across the road.

cloze.template:
Receptionist: Good morning, Greenwood Medical Centre. How can I help you?
Patient: Hello, I'd like to make an {{b1}} to see a doctor, please.
Receptionist: Of course. Are you a registered {{b2}} with us?
Patient: Yes, I am. My name is Sarah Browne. B-R-O-W-N-E.
Receptionist: Thank you, I can see your details here. Can I ask what the appointment is for?
Patient: I've had a {{b3}} throat and a headache for the past three days. I'm also feeling quite tired.
Receptionist: I'm sorry to hear that. Is this {{b4}}, or would a routine appointment be fine?
Patient: It's not an emergency, but I'd rather not wait too long.
Receptionist: Understood. We have an available {{b5}} tomorrow at ten fifteen in the morning. Would that work for you?
Patient: Tomorrow morning is perfect. Which doctor will I be seeing?
Receptionist: You'll be seeing Dr {{b6}}. She's very good.
Patient: That's fine. Is there anything I should bring with me?
Receptionist: Just your patient ID {{b7}} if you have it. If not, we can look you up on the system.
Patient: Great, I'll bring it. And where exactly is the clinic? I've never been before.
Receptionist: We're at fourteen Oak Road, just {{b8}} the post office.
Patient: Perfect. Thank you so much for your {{b9}}.
Receptionist: You're welcome. I hope you feel {{b10}} soon. See you tomorrow at ten {{b11}}.

blanks.explanation: 'appointment' — a scheduled time to see a doctor is called an appointment.
'patient' — someone who receives medical care is a patient.
'sore' — a sore throat is a painful or irritated throat.
'urgent' — the receptionist asks if the appointment is urgent, meaning whether it needs to happen immediately.
'slot' — an available slot is an open time for an appointment.
'Patel' — the receptionist tells the patient she will see Dr Patel.
'card' — a patient ID card is used to confirm identity at the clinic.
'opposite' — 'just opposite' means directly facing or across the road from.
'help' — 'thank you for your help' is a standard polite expression.
'better' — 'feel better' is the standard expression used when someone is unwell.
'fifteen' — the appointment is at ten fifteen (10:15).

criticalThinkingQuestion: In this conversation, the patient is able to book an appointment the next day. In some countries, people wait weeks or even months to see a doctor. Do you think healthcare should be free and available to everyone equally, or is it acceptable for people to pay for faster or better care? What are the advantages and disadvantages of each system?
